zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Le Grys於1248年出生在英國諾福爾可明日香時，他的父親Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight36歲，母親Lady Le Grys33歲。他於1278年與Mrs. Olyve Le Grys在英國諾福爾可明日香結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他埋葬在英國諾福爾可明日香。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Le Grys于1248年出生在英国諾福爾可明日香时，他的父亲Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight36岁，母亲Lady Le Grys33岁。他于1278年与Mrs. Olyve Le Grys在英国諾福爾可明日香结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他埋葬在英国諾福爾可明日香。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Grys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Durham): variant of Gray , with post-medieval excrescent -s, or of Grace .

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Le Grys was born in 1248, in Langley, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight, was 36 and his mother, Lady Le Grys, was 33. He married Mrs. Olyve Le Grys in 1278, in Langley, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He was buried in Langley, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Le Grys est né en 1248, à Langley, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Lady Le Grys, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Olyve Le Grys en 1278, à Langley, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Ilenterré à Langley, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Le Grys 1248 in Langley, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight 36 und seine Mutter Lady Le Grys 33. Er hat 1278 in Langley, Norfolk, England Mrs. Olyve Le Grys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er wurde in Langley, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Le Grys è nato il nel 1248 nel Langley, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Lady Le Grys, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Olyve Le Grys nel 1278, nel Langley, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Fu sepolto a Langley, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Le Grysが1248年にイギリスノーフォルクラングリーで生まれた時，父Sir. Symon Le Grys Knightは36歳，母Lady Le Grysは33歳であった。1278年にイギリスノーフォルクラングリーでMrs. Olyve Le Grysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼はイギリスノーフォルクラングリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Le Grys(이)가 1248년 영국 노르폴크 랭글리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Le Grys(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1278년 영국 노르폴크 랭글리에서 Mrs. Olyve Le Grys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 노르폴크 랭글리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Le Grys nasceu em 1248, em Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Lady Le Grys, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Olyve Le Grys em 1278, em Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele foi sepultado em Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Le Grys родился в 1248 г. в местности лангли, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Le Grys, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Olyve Le Grys в 1278 г. в местности лангли, норфолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он был похоронен в местности лангли, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Le Grys nació en 1248, en Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir. Symon Le Grys Knight, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Lady Le Grys, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Mrs. Olyve Le Grys en 1278, en Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Fue sepultado en Langley, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
